expectations.text: Tri-driver pattern: auto-detects Neon serverless vs Neon HTTP vs local pg based on URL and NEXT_RUNTIME
expectations.evidence: db/index.ts only uses `@neondatabase/serverless` with `drizzle-orm/neon-http`. There is no detection of NEXT_RUNTIME, no switching between neon-serverless (WebSocket) vs neon-http, and no local `pg` driver path. The implementation hardcodes a single driver strategy.

expectations.text: Lazy proxy pattern: db instance doesn't eagerly connect on import
expectations.evidence: db/index.ts creates the neon client and drizzle instance at module scope: `const sql = neon(process.env.DATABASE_URL); export const db = drizzle(sql, { schema });`. There is no Proxy, no lazy initialization, and no deferred connection. The connection is eagerly established on import.

expectations.text: casing: 'snake_case' set in BOTH drizzle.config.ts AND client initialization
expectations.evidence: drizzle.config.ts has no `casing` property — it only sets schema, out, dialect, and dbCredentials. db/index.ts calls `drizzle(sql, { schema })` with no `casing` option. Neither location specifies `casing: 'snake_case'`.
The directory packages/nextjs/services/database/ exists and contains api.ts, hooks.ts, index.ts, and types.ts. Verified via both filesystem listing and reading the actual file contents.

The services/database/ layer is a client-side API service (fetch wrappers around /api/users endpoints) with React Query hooks, not a repository pattern. The API routes in app/api/users/ call drizzle directly — there is no repository abstraction layer (e.g., a UserRepository class/module that encapsulates database queries). The db access is inlined in route handlers.

expectations.text: Root package.json has proxy scripts (drizzle-kit, db:seed, db:wipe)
expectations.evidence: The root package.json has no drizzle-related scripts at all. The db:* scripts (db:generate, db:migrate, db:seed, db:studio, db:push) are only in packages/nextjs/package.json. There is no db:wipe script anywhere, and no root-level proxy scripts for drizzle-kit or db:seed.

expectations.text: Docker Compose for local PostgreSQL development
expectations.evidence: No docker-compose file exists in the project (outside of node_modules). The only docker-compose files found are in packages/nextjs/node_modules/bgipfs/templates/ which are unrelated. The setup instructions reference only Neon cloud, not local PostgreSQL via Docker.

No .env.development file exists anywhere in the worktree. The .env.example file was modified to add DATABASE_URL, and the summary.md instructions reference `.env.local` for setup. The SE-2 convention of .env.development was not followed.

Grep for PRODUCTION_DATABASE_HOSTNAME across the entire worktree returned no matches. There is no production safety guard checking the database hostname before destructive operations like seeding or wiping.

expectations.text: All required dependencies in correct locations (drizzle-orm, @neondatabase/serverless, pg, dotenv, drizzle-kit, drizzle-seed, tsx, @types/pg)
expectations.evidence: packages/nextjs/package.json has drizzle-orm (dep), @neondatabase/serverless (dep), and drizzle-kit (devDep). Missing from package.json: pg, dotenv, drizzle-seed, tsx, @types/pg. The seed script uses `npx tsx` rather than declaring tsx as a dependency. Only 3 of 8 required dependencies are present.

claims.claim: Database connection singleton using Neon serverless driver
claims.evidence: While the db instance is created once at module scope, it is not a true singleton pattern (no getInstance(), no null-check). It's a simple module-level export that eagerly connects.

claims.claim: Full CRUD support via API routes
claims.evidence: API routes at /api/users (GET, POST) and /api/users/[address] (GET, PUT, DELETE) exist and the services/database/api.ts provides corresponding fetch wrappers.

claims.claim: React Query-based hooks for data fetching with automatic cache invalidation
claims.evidence: hooks.ts uses @tanstack/react-query with useQuery and useMutation, and mutation hooks call queryClient.invalidateQueries on success.

Uses neon-http driver which is edge-compatible, but the eager module-level initialization with a throw on missing DATABASE_URL would cause issues in edge/serverless cold starts where the env var check runs at import time regardless of whether the module is needed.

suggestions.reason: This assertion is somewhat ambiguous — it could be interpreted broadly (any structured data access layer) or strictly (a Repository class encapsulating queries). The services/database/ layer could be argued as a form of repository if the bar is 'organized data access'. Consider clarifying whether the expectation means a server-side repository wrapping drizzle queries, or if the API service layer counts.

This assertion passed but the files at services/database/ are client-side fetch wrappers, not the database connection/query layer. The actual database code lives at db/. The assertion only checks path existence, not whether database logic resides there. A stronger assertion would verify that the database connection and query logic (not just API wrappers) lives under services/database/.
No assertion checks whether the API routes actually compile or handle errors correctly. The implementation creates routes but their correctness is not verified.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions are well-targeted at specific architectural patterns (tri-driver, lazy proxy, casing convention) that require genuine understanding of Drizzle+Neon best practices. The without-skill run missed almost all of them (1/10 pass), which shows good discrimination. The services/database/ assertion could be tightened to ensure the right kind of code lives there.
